source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 13.4-13.6 hostname and resolution (pp. 244-251)

questions.q: Permanently set the machine's hostname to HOME10.
questions.why: hostnamectl set-hostname changes the hostname permanently (it is the systemd replacement for the hostname command).

questions.q: Display the hostname information (static hostname, OS, kernel, etc.).
questions.why: hostnamectl with no arguments queries the current hostname and system details.

questions.q: In what order does the OS resolve a hostname?
options: DNS cache, then /etc/hosts, then the DNS servers
DNS servers, then /etc/hosts, then the cache
questions.why: Resolution checks the DNS cache first, then the local /etc/hosts file, then queries the configured DNS servers.

questions.q: What is the format of a line in /etc/hosts?
options: IP address, then hostname (required), then an optional alias
hostname, then IP address, then netmask
domain, then zone, then record type
questions.why: Each line is: IP (required), hostname (required), then optional alias(es) - e.g. 192.168.1.10 rockstar.rockylinux.lan rockstar.

questions.q: How should you change the DNS servers in /etc/resolv.conf on Rocky 8/9/10?
options: Don't edit it directly - configure DNS with NetworkManager (e.g. nmcli connection modify ... ipv4.dns ...)
Edit /etc/resolv.conf by hand; it is authoritative
questions.why: /etc/resolv.conf is generated by NetworkManager; set the nameservers via nmcli rather than editing the file directly.

questions.q: In /etc/nsswitch.conf, what does the line 'hosts: files dns myhostname' mean?
options: Resolve host names using the local files (/etc/hosts) first, then DNS, then the special myhostname method
questions.why: NSS tries the methods left to right: files (/etc/hosts), then dns, then myhostname (local resolution of the machine's own name).

questions.q: Look up rockylinux.org through NSSwitch (files + DNS) using getent.
questions.why: getent queries the NSSwitch databases, so 'getent hosts' honours /etc/hosts as well as DNS (unlike a pure DNS lookup).

questions.q: Set the IPv4 DNS servers for connection ens160 to 114.114.114.114 and 8.8.8.8 with nmcli.
questions.why: nmcli sets the nameservers; NetworkManager then regenerates /etc/resolv.conf (restart NetworkManager to apply).
